section.title: Overview
section.p: This is a build distribution. Unlike a checkout of the git repository, it ships the generated source and the rendered documentation pre-built, so can be built and installed without the code generation or the documentation tooling.

list.list-item: src -- source, including the pre-generated *.auto.c.inc files
test -- smoke test to verify the build

section.title: Build
section.p: builds with meson and ninja. The required libraries are libpq, OpenSSL (>= 1.1.1), libxml2, lz4, bz2, and zlib; zstd, libssh2 (for SFTP), and libsystemd are optional. For example, on Debian/Ubuntu:
section.code-block: meson setup build . ninja -C build meson install -C build
section.p: The pgbackrest binary is built at build/src/pgbackrest. See the user guide for detailed build instructions, including dependencies for other operating systems.

section.title: Test
section.p: A smoke test is included to verify that the build works. It finds each installation on the system and runs the newly-built pgbackrest through a backup and restore cycle against every supported version found: create a cluster, configure archiving, create the stanza, check, back up, restore, and verify that the restored data matches. Versions that does not support are reported and skipped. The test requires nothing beyond the build environment and -- no additional Python packages.
Run it with meson after building:
Add -v to stream the output of each step as it runs:
is searched for in the standard packaging paths:

Each version is tested in a temporary directory with its own repository and a private unix socket, so a running cluster is not disturbed. The test fails when there is no to test against, since a test that silently does nothing does not verify the build. Since will not run as root, running the test as root drops to the postgres user and fails if that user does not exist.
The test can also be run directly, which allows the defaults to be overridden:

Useful options are --pg-bin-path to search an additional path for , --version to test only a specific version, and --user to select the user to drop to when running as root. Use --help for the complete list.

section.title: Documentation
section.p: The rendered documentation is in doc: the command reference in doc/man and the HTML documentation in doc/html.

Two user guides are provided, Debian/Ubuntu and RHEL/Rocky/Alma. If only one user guide is required, replace user-guide-index.html with the desired user guide. For example, to keep only the Debian/Ubuntu user guide:
